channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump administration to refer Maine to Justice Department over transgender participation in sports
item.description: PORTLAND, Maine (AP) — The deadline arrived Friday for Maine officials to reach a resolution with the U.S. Education Department over a finding that the state violated antidiscrimination laws by allowing transgender athletes to participate in girls’ sports.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Supreme Court rules Trump administration must 'facilitate’ return of mistakenly deported man
item.description: The court’s emergency ruling instructs the lower judge to clarify the language of her order but said she acted properly in ensuring that Kilmar Abrego Garcia’s case is handled “as it would have been had he not been improperly sent to El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WGN 9

item.title: Spanish family and pilot killed in NYC sightseeing helicopter crash
item.description: Photos posted on the helicopter company’s website showed the couple and their children smiling as they boarded just before the flight took off.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, KRON4

item.title: Florida assistant principal arrested after making 13-year-old student rub her feet 'like he loved her': police
item.description: An assistant principal at a Florida middle school is facing multiple charges after she allegedly made a 13-year-old male student rub her feet “like he loved her," authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NYC sightseeing helicopter plunges into river, killing 6 including Spanish family and pilot
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A New York City sightseeing helicopter broke apart in midair Thursday and crashed upside-down into the Hudson River, killing the pilot and a family of five Spanish tourists in the latest U.S. aviation disaster, officials said.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, JENNIFER PELTZ and TED SHAFFREY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Judge will decide whether to proceed with Menendez brothers resentencing hearing
item.description: A judge is set to decide whether to proceed with the resentencing hearings for Erik and Lyle Menendez in light of the Los Angeles County district attorney opposing their release.
item.dc:creator: Jaimie Ding, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: In fight over insurance, neighbors crowdsource LA fire contamination data
item.description: Los Angeles-area residents whose homes survived the Eaton wildfire in January face a whole different set of struggles to their neighbors whose homes were burned to the ground.
item.dc:creator: Claudia Lauer And Sally Ho, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The Latest: Supreme Court tells Trump administration to facilitate return of mistakenly deported man
item.description: The Supreme Court on Thursday said President Donald Trump’s administration must facilitate the return of Kilmar Abrego Garcia, a Maryland man who was mistakenly deported to El Salvador, rejecting the administration’s emergency appeal.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Near record heat builds across Utah ahead of cooldown
item.description: The heat is on across the Beehive State for the next few days with cooler weather forecasted to set in Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Alana Brophy, ABC 4

item.title: New York bills would protect Tesla owners, target Tesla dominance
item.description: That measure follows nationwide reports of Tesla owners, dealerships, and charging stations being vandalized.
item.dc:creator: Johan Sheridan, NewsNation

item.title: Movie theaters erupt in chaos as part of 'Minecraft' social media trend
item.description: Rowdy crowds of young people have been disrupting screenings of the newly released "A Minecraft Movie" around the country as they take part in the so-called "chicken jockey" trend.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, KRON4

item.title: US egg prices increase to record high, dashing hopes of cheap eggs by Easter
item.description: U.S. egg prices increased again last month to reach a new record-high of $6.23 per dozen despite President Donald Trump’s predictions, a drop in wholesale prices and no egg farms having bird flu outbreaks.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK and DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Stocks slide as Trump's trade war with China spooks Wall Street
item.description: Stocks slid Thursday as President Trump’s escalating trade war with China and concerns about its economic implications continued to rattle Wall Street.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Report: Drug lord linked to DEA agent’s death freed
item.description: One of the three leaders of the Guadalajara cartel linked to the torture-slaying of Drug Enforcement Administration Agent Enrique “Kiki” Camarena in 1985 has been released in Mexico, according to news reports.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, Fox 5

item.title: Helicopter crashes in New York City's Hudson River; 6 dead
item.description: A Bell 206 helicopter belonging to a tour company crashed in the water near Jersey City and Hoboken around 3:15 p.m., according to NYPD Commissioner Jessica Tisch.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Foster mom accused of exchanging child for exotic animal in bizarre trade
item.description: Brenda Deutsch is facing multiple felony charges after Missouri prosecutors say she allegedly tried to trade a foster child in her care for an "exotic animal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gun discharges in student's backpack at Maryland elementary school, officials say
item.description: The student had allegedly brought the gun from home but shared that they had no intentions of hurting anyone, according to CCPS.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Gable, NewsNation

item.title: 4 West Virginia University students, 3 alumni have visas revoked by US government
item.description: WVU's Director Johnson said that in every case, the government's justification was that the person's name was "found in a criminal records check.
item.dc:creator: Joey Rather, NewsNation

item.title: Hearing looks at rules for evidence and other details in Bryan Kohberger's quadruple murder trial
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — Prosecutors and attorneys for a man charged in the killings of four University of Idaho students in 2022 argued some of the final ground rules they want for Bryan Kohberger's trial in a hearing Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hundreds of students, dozens of colleges hit by Trump's visa purge: What to know
item.description: Hundreds of foreign students at dozens of colleges across the country have had their higher education experience turned upside down as the Trump administration has expanded its immigration crackdown beyond those involved in the pro-Palestinian protests.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Homeland Security agents denied entry at two Los Angeles schools
item.description: It is believed to be the first time federal agents tried to enter Los Angeles schools since President Trump announced a crackdown on immigration.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, Fox 5

item.title: Lawmakers say airplanes bumped each other on runway at Reagan National
item.description: Multiple lawmakers on Thursday said their airplane was bumped by another plane while waiting on the runway at Reagan National Airport.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: American Airlines flight carrying US lawmakers ‘bumped’ by second plane on tarmac in DC
item.description: Several U.S. lawmakers on Thursday said a plane clipped their aircraft while awaiting takeoff at Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: LA County homeless spending under the microscope of newly appointed US attorney
item.description: Newly appointed U.S. Attorney Bill Essayli has launched a federal task force to investigate billions in unaccounted homeless funds in Los Angeles County.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Playground demolition conversation evolves into how to address homelessness
item.description: "If I had a child I would not let them play there because they might find hypodermic needles or something else," said one resident.
item.dc:creator: Paul Steeno, NewsNation

item.title: Giant bust of Elon Musk's head vandalized near Starbase in Texas
item.description: "We don't want this to happen again because it was an effort to put it up and very expensive," the owner said.
item.dc:creator: Mia Morales, NewsNation

item.title: Bette Davis’ historic Connecticut home for sale
item.description: The legendary actress's Westport, Connecticut, home has come up for sale in a once-in-a-lifetime listing.
item.dc:creator: Paula Froelich, NewsNation

item.title: Education Department withdraws from plan to address discipline disparities for Native students
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Education Department has withdrawn from an agreement to address disparities in discipline for Native American students at a South Dakota school system, saying it was wrongly rooted in efforts to promote diversity, equity and inclusion.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA and SARAH RAZA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Egg prices jump to record high despite Trump's predictions and bird flu slowing
item.description: The increase reported Thursday in the Consumer Price Index means consumers and businesses that rely on eggs should not anticipate immediate relief.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Jury awards $1.68 billion to women who accused writer and director James Toback of sexual abuse
item.description: The judgement marks one of the largest jury awards since the advent of the #MeToo movement, as well as in New York state history
item.dc:creator: Susan Haigh, Fox 5

item.title: This federal rule helped clear air over America's most beloved parks. Trump's EPA wants to kill it
item.description: During a hike in the Great Smoky Mountains National Park in 1995, Don Barger climbed Chilhowee Mountain hoping to gaze across the valley below. All he saw was a wall of gray haze. Today, he said, he can see some 50 miles (80 kilometers) across that same valley to the Cumberland Mountains.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND and MARY KATHERINE WILDEMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Ballerina Ksenia Karelina freed from Russian custody in prisoner exchange
item.description: Ksenia Karelina, a U.S.-Russian dual national who was detained last year on treason charges, was released from Russia in the latest prisoner exchange, according to the State Department.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: Sweepstakes giant Publishers Clearing House files for bankruptcy
item.description: Publishers Clearing House, famous for its big checks and sweepstakes, files for Chapter 11 bankruptcy as it shifts to digital advertising.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, WGN 9

item.title: Local businesses react to Trump's tariffs, prepare for potential long-term effects
item.description: Local businesses are feeling anxiety and uncertainty around President Donald Trump’s tariffs — despite the president backing down on his tariffs on most nations for 90 days today.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan May, ABC 4

item.title: Exclusive: Pence agrees Trump’s tariff policy a potential midterm liability for GOP
item.description: Former Vice President Mike Pence said the Trump administration’s aggressive use of tariffs poses a potential risk for Republicans in the midterm elections, even as he expressed relief about a walk-back of some of the most severe tariffs on allies.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Tips for spring gardening: Utah experts talk best times to prep and plant
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4) -- Gardening can be many things to different people—sometimes a chore, other times a passion. If you're one of those passionate gardeners or someone looking to get started, here are some tips from experts at Millcreek Gardens to help you get your garden started.
item.dc:creator: Nate Larsen, ABC 4

item.title: Trump touts Supreme Court deportation ruling as a major victory, but legal fight is far from over
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration is touting a Supreme Court ruling allowing it to resume deportations under the Alien Enemies Act as a major victory, but the immigration fight is far from over.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, ABC 4
